key_insight: A hybrid DFT + machine-learning workflow can rapidly rank equimolar NaCl-type entropy-stabilized oxide compositions by predicting convex-hull stability across thousands of candidate quinary mixtures.
engineering_relevance: This enables much faster experimental down-selection of promising multicomponent oxide compositions, reducing the need for exhaustive first-principles screening.
method: Build a reference database of binary and ternary ordered/disordered oxides using GGA and meta-GGA DFT.
Represent quinary compositions with special quasirandom structure (SQS) supercells and compute convex-hull distance and stabilization temperature.
Train supervised models, with an optimized multilayer perceptron selected as the best performer for screening 4368 equimolar quinary systems.
evidence_signal: The best model achieved about 4 kJ/mol test error while requiring explicit DFT calculations for only roughly 10% of the quinary compositions.
watch_item: Predicted stabilization temperatures still inherit systematic thermodynamic approximation errors, so absolute values should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Data-Driven Prediction of NaCl-Type Entropy-Stabilized Oxide Compositions from First-Principles and Supervised Learning
